definition: The apparent contradiction of simultaneous rate cuts and QT is coherent when decomposed through channels: rate cuts support income flows via the Kalecki channel (debt service relief on floating-rate obligations) while QT restructures portfolio composition via the Tobin channel (duration return to private portfolios). These are complementary instruments operating on different margins, not contradictory signals.
evidence: Rate cuts = Kalecki channel (income flow support via debt service relief)
QT = Tobin channel (portfolio composition via duration return to private portfolios)
Complementary instruments operating on different margins, not contradictory
Validated as genuinely useful analytical contribution in pair_2 debate
No agent contested the decomposition when presented
open_questions: Which channel dominates for which asset class?
Does the income channel offset or merely delay the portfolio channel's effects?
How should factor models handle this mixed-signal regime with no historical analog?
What is the net effect on different points of the yield curve?
